message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="HTMX review of Waiting For design - approved">
Luca, excellent design proposal. Here's my review from the HTMX/hypermedia side:

**Route structure: APPROVED.** The routes follow our established conventions perfectly:
- GET for reading (list page, edit form, single item fragment)
- POST for mutations (add, complete, delete, edit)
- Same URL pattern as projects (`/waiting-for/{id}/complete`, `/waiting-for/{id}/edit`, etc.)
- The `GET /waiting-for/{id}` endpoint for the cancel-edit flow is good -- we've used this pattern on projects and it works well.

**HTMX interaction design: One clarification.**

For `POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete` -- the route name says "complete" but the user-facing label is "Received." I think using `/complete` in the URL is fine since "complete" is our internal convention (consistent with projects and next actions), while "Received" is the user-facing label on the button. Just want to confirm that's the intent. If the team prefers `/resolve` in the URL to match GTD language more closely, I'm fine either way -- it's an internal implementation detail.

**Add form with two fields: Good approach.** The add form needs:
- `hx-post="/waiting-for"` with `hx-target="#waiting-for-list"` and `hx-swap="beforeend"`
- Two visible `<label>` elements for the two inputs
- `hx-on::after-request` to clear both inputs and refocus the first on success
- Both fields submitted as a standard form POST for no-JS fallback

One thought on the form layout: the two fields (title + waiting_on) could be on separate lines or in a row. I'd suggest separate lines for mobile-first, since two text inputs side by side gets cramped on small screens. Steve Schoger can weigh in on the visual layout.

**Progressive enhancement: Confirmed.** All the mutation endpoints should follow our dual-response pattern:
- HTMX request -> return fragment + HX-Trigger announce
- Non-HTMX request -> redirect to `/waiting-for`
- This is the same pattern used in projects.rs and next_actions.rs, so no surprises.

**Deferring convert-to-next-action: +1.** This is a cross-list operation that needs context selection UI. Keeping it out of the initial CRUD commit is the right call -- it keeps the first commit atomic and shippable. We can add it as a follow-up (possibly part of Step 9 or a separate small commit).

**No concerns.** This design is consistent with our established hypermedia patterns. Ready for you to start coding.

</teammate-message>

input.content:
Thanks Carson. To confirm your clarification:

**`/complete` URL vs "Received" label** -- Yes, that's the intent. `/complete` is the internal convention matching Projects and NextActions (both use `/complete`). "Received" is the user-facing button label. Keeping the URL consistent with our established pattern is more important than matching GTD vocabulary in the URL path, since users never see the URL directly.

**Two-field form layout** -- Implemented as separate lines (stacked), exactly as you suggested for mobile-first. Title field gets full width, person field + Add button on a second row. See `templates/waiting_for.html` lines 26-54.

**All HTMX patterns confirmed as implemented:**
- `hx-post="/waiting-for"` with `hx-target="#waiting-for-list"` and `hx-swap="beforeend"` on the add form
- `hx-on::after-request` clears both inputs and refocuses the title input
- Dual-response pattern in every handler (HTMX -> fragment + HX-Trigger, non-HTMX -> redirect)
- `htmx_response_with_announce()` for all mutations

Thanks for the approval. No blocking issues from your review.
input.summary: Confirming Carson's HTMX review points, no blockers

routing.content:
Thanks Carson. To confirm your clarification:

**`/complete` URL vs "Received" label** -- Yes, that's the intent. `/complete` is the internal convention matching Projects and NextActions (both use `/complete`). "Received" is the user-facing button label. Keeping the URL consistent with our established pattern is more important than matching GTD vocabulary in the URL path, since users never see the URL directly.

**Two-field form layout** -- Implemented as separate lines (stacked), exactly as you suggested for mobile-first. Title field gets full width, person field + Add button on a second row. See `templates/waiting_for.html` lines 26-54.

**All HTMX patterns confirmed as implemented:**
- `hx-post="/waiting-for"` with `hx-target="#waiting-for-list"` and `hx-swap="beforeend"` on the add form
- `hx-on::after-request` clears both inputs and refocuses the title input
- Dual-response pattern in every handler (HTMX -> fragment + HX-Trigger, non-HTMX -> redirect)
- `htmx_response_with_announce()` for all mutations

Thanks for the approval. No blocking issues from your review.

content.text:
Carson approves with no blockers. Current review status:
- Steve Krug: APPROVED (no blockers, 3 minor suggestions addressed)
- Carson Gross: APPROVED (no concerns)
- Kent Beck, Scott Wlaschin, Lea Verou, Steve Schoger, Heydon Pickering, Marty Cagan: awaiting feedback
